verified.notes: Utah Medicaid is a multi-program system with varying eligibility rules by category. Age 19 is the minimum for Adult Expansion Medicaid (authorized 12/23/2019, up to 138% FPL). Other categories (children, pregnant women, elderly, disabled) have different age requirements and income thresholds. Asset limits not specified in provided official sources. Income limits vary by household size and category (55%-133% FPL depending on category per https://medicaid.utah.gov/who-eligible/). Phone number provided is for Health Program Representatives regarding benefits. Effective fall 2026, citizenship requirements will change per OBBBA bill (https://medicaid.utah.gov/obbba/).

verified.notes: Program has no income requirement or asset limits. Minimum age is 18 for most caregivers, though older relatives (55+) can be caregivers for children under 18 or adults 18-59 with disabilities. Care recipients must be 60+, any age with Alzheimer's/related neurological disorder, or disabled individuals 18+. Phone number from MAG (Mountainland Association of Governments) official page; also confirmed on state DAAS website.
